title: Why does onchain perp trading still feel clunky even with good liquidity?
author: user_alpha231
selftext:
Speed and liquidity on perp DEXs have improved a lot over the last year, but trading still feels fragmented and inconsistent across venues.

Two platforms can look almost identical on the Ul and behave completely differently when it comes to fills, latency, and routing.

It's starting to feel like coordination at the execution layer is the real bottleneck now, not infra speed or liquidity. Routing, settlement paths, and latency domains seem to matter way more than most people realise.

Curious how others here see this. Is this also your experience, or am I missing something?

replies.author: user_alpha231
replies.body:
Not calling out any one DEX in particular – more the general pattern across the big ones (HL, Drift, GMX, etc).

On paper they all look fast and liquid, but once you start trading size you notice differences in fills, slippage, and how orders actually get routed. That’s more what I’m getting at.

replies.author: StevenVinyl
replies.body:
when you get a bad fill on HL it's generally insane.   
Also when Arb gets crowded and trading vol. spikes. I've gotten quotes for 700$ to open/close a position (market) on GMX lol.
